programs.name: Choices for Care (CFC) Waiver
programs.what_it_does: The Choices for Care waiver provides long-term services and supports to eligible seniors to avoid or delay nursing home placement. It offers home and community-based services as an alternative to institutional care.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Vermont seniors needing nursing home level of care, aged 65 or older, or younger with disabilities.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Long-term services and supports to remain at home instead of nursing home; value varies by individual needs.[1]

programs.name: Medicaid for the Aged, Blind and Disabled (ABD)
programs.what_it_does: Provides health coverage for low-income seniors and disabled individuals. Covers medical services beyond standard Medicaid for this population.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+, blind, or disabled Vermonters meeting income and resource limits.[1][4]
programs.benefit_value: Full Medicaid health coverage including long-term care services; premium-free for eligible.[4]

programs.name: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings. Coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid covered services plus additional support.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors aged 55+ who can live safely in the community with services.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care; capitation model covers full needs (limited availability in VT; search results do not confirm VT-specific program)

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualified Individual (QI)
programs.what_it_does: QMB pays Medicare Part A/B premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance. SLMB and QI help pay Part B premiums for low-income Medicare beneficiaries.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Vermont Medicare enrollees meeting income guidelines (no resource limits in VT); U.S. citizens or legal residents.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Full premium/deductible coverage (QMB); Part B premium only (SLMB/QI); 3 of 4 MSPs available.[1]

programs.name: 3SquaresVT
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly EBT benefits to supplement food purchases at stores and markets. Features simplified application (3SquaresVT IN A SNAP) for seniors 60+ and disabled.[3][5]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Vermonters aged 60+ or disabled; households meeting income guidelines.[3][5]
programs.benefit_value: Average $180/month for older Vermonter living alone.[5]

programs.name: Fuel Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Helps low-income households pay heating costs during winter. Provides one-time payments to fuel vendors.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Vermont households including seniors; apply via myBenefits portal.[2][5]
programs.benefit_value: One-time annual payment based on income, household size, fuel type; amount varies.[2]

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program
programs.what_it_does: Improves energy efficiency of homes through insulation, sealing, and upgrades. Reduces heating costs for low-income households.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Vermont homeowners/renters including seniors; prioritized for vulnerable groups.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements; long-term energy savings (specific $ value not in results).[7]

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free counseling on Medicare enrollment, plans, and appeals. Provides referrals for home care and long-term services.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Vermont Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors; available via AAAs.[4]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors. Often provided through Area Agencies on Aging.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound Vermont seniors unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: Daily/weekly meal delivery; often low/no cost based on income (details via 800-642-5119).[1]

programs.name: Caregiver Respite Support
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for family caregivers through in-home or facility care. Part of CFC waiver and AAA services.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of CFC-eligible seniors needing nursing home level care.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Respite services covered under waiver; hours/value per care plan (via AAA helpline).[1]

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid work training at community sites. Helps unemployed low-income seniors gain skills and employment.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage training positions ~20 hrs/week (federal program; VT administered via AAA).[1]

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal help for income-eligible seniors on benefits, housing, abuse. Available through Legal Aid or AAA referrals.[2]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Vermont seniors facing legal issues.[2]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living. Investigates complaints and ensures quality care.
programs.who_its_for: Residents/family in VT long-term care facilities; access via AAAs.[4]

programs.name: Vermont Senior Companion Program
programs.what_it_does: This statewide program matches volunteers aged 55 and older with older neighbors needing companionship and assistance to help them stay in their homes longer. It provides support for daily tasks like grocery shopping and doctor visits, offers respite for family caregivers, and combats loneliness and isolation.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Volunteers aged 55+ with yearly income under $30,120 (single) or $40,880 (couple), serving 10 hours/week; serves older adults needing companionship.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Statewide volunteer matching program specifically tailored for Vermont's older adults, funded through AmeriCorps Seniors but operated locally by organizations like Central Vermont Council on Aging.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Non-monetary: companionship, assistance with daily tasks, and caregiver respite; volunteers receive stipends (not specified in source).
